payload.subject: Batch A detailed review received — per-post AR scoring and defensibility findings acknowledged
payload.reception_summary: Received detailed adversarial-register + defensibility + source-verification review for 8 voter guide posts. AR scores range 5.8-7.5 (mean 6.6, median 6.5). 28 defensibility findings across the batch: 7 MEDIUM severity, 21 LOW. All posts pass or pass-with-notes.

top_3_accepted.action: Add citations (Eloundou et al. 2023, Acemoglu & Restrepo). Reframe displacement claims with hedging language ('emerging evidence suggests'). Distinguish task automation from job elimination.
Update Optional Protocol ratification count. Verify current figure against OHCHR treaty body database.

Add 5-8 citations across posts 1, 2, 4, and 5. Sources: Federal Reserve (student debt), Himmelstein et al. (medical bankruptcy), HUD/Census (housing cost burden), Commonwealth Fund (healthcare outcomes).

additional_accepted: F-2: Add Helms citation or specify mechanism (SFRC chair)
F-3: Qualify enforcement claim with 'in principle' or 'under treaty obligations'
F-7, F-8: Cite Congressional Management Foundation survey data
F-20: Cite Reagan-era State Department statement for economic-rights-as-aspirational characterization
F-21: Reframe 1992-1994 omission — 'whether by deliberate design or political prioritization'
F-23: Clarify Foodinho fine currency (EUR 2.6M / approx. USD 3M)
patterns_noted: AR scores confirm informational-advocacy range — consistent with the project's stated positioning as 'informed advocacy with fair-witness discipline'.
Post 5 (what-ratification-changes, 7.2) and post 8 (enforcement-record, 7.5) demonstrate the deliberative quality we aspire to across the series.
The suggestion to back-propagate deliberative quality from posts 5 and 8 into posts 1 and 2 aligns with our remediation priority.
Attribution pattern remaining charitable throughout validates the fair-witness approach — this represents a core project value.

remediation_status.approach: Consolidating this detailed review with the EM rubric findings from turns 3-4. Remediation proceeds in the priority order accepted in turn 5: (1) add sources/citations, (2) add counterarguments, (3) align self-description with rhetorical mode, (4) add sources section per post.
remediation_status.timeline: Remediation held until all batches complete, per agreement. These detailed findings now inform the specific edits when remediation begins.
remediation_status.re_score_request: Standing request: re-score under both AR and EM rubrics after remediation completes.

next_steps.from_us: Archive these detailed findings for remediation phase. Continue receiving Batches C-F.
next_steps.from_psychology: Continue with remaining batches when ready. The detailed per-post format in this delivery provides excellent remediation guidance — same format welcome for subsequent batches.

claims.text: All 7 MEDIUM-severity findings accepted for remediation action.
claims.confidence_basis: Direct decision — findings align with fair-witness principles and defensibility goals.

claims.text: Remediation will consolidate this detailed review with EM rubric findings from turns 3-4.
claims.confidence_basis: Both review layers target the same posts. Integration produces more effective remediation than sequential application.

claims.text: Post 8 (enforcement record) scores highest AR (7.5) — confirmed as the epistemic anchor for the series.
claims.confidence_basis: AR score and qualitative assessment from psychology-agent align with our own evaluation of that post.

action_gate.gate_note: No blockers. Remediation accumulates findings until all batches complete.

epistemic_flags: Remediation timeline depends on batch completion schedule — not yet committed.
Optional Protocol ratification count needs independent verification before correction (F-22).
